          h₅ ≪ f ≈ h₅ ≪ f ≪ (g ≪ f) := by
            simp [h₇]
          _ ≈ (h₅ ≪ f) ≪ g ≪ f := by
            simp [assoc]
          _ ≈ (h₆ ≪ f) ≪ g ≪ f := by
            simp [h₇]
          _ ≈ h₆ ≪ f ≪ (g ≪ f) := by simp [assoc]
          _ ≈ h₆ ≪ f := by simp [h₇]
      have h₉ : h₅ ≪ f ≈ h₆ ≪ f := h₈
      have h₁₀ : h₅ ≈ h₆ := hm h₉
      exact h₁₀
    -- Show that g ≪ f ≈ 1 using the fact that g is monic and the composition is invertible.
    have h₅ : (g ≪ f) ≪ g ≈ g := by
      calc
        (g ≪ f) ≪ g ≈ g ≪ (f ≪ g) := by simp [assoc]
        _ ≈ g ≪ 1 := by simp [h₂]
        _ ≈ g := by simp
    have h₆ : g ≪ 1 ≈ g := by simp
    have h₇ : (g ≪ f) ≪ g ≈ g ≪ 1 := by
      calc
        (g ≪ f) ≪ g ≈ g := h₅
        _ ≈ g ≪ 1 := by simp [h₆]
        _ ≈ g ≪ 1 := by simp [h₆]
    have h₈ : g ≪ f ≈ 1 := by
      apply h₄
      simpa [assoc] using h₇
    exact h₈
  -- Prove that f is an isomorphism using the inverses f⁻¹ = g and g⁻¹ = f.
  refine' ⟨g, _, _⟩
  <;> simp_all [Inverse]
  <;>
  (try simp_all [assoc]) <;>
  (try simp_all [h₂, h₃]) <;>
  (try simp_all [hm]) <;>
  (try simp_all [he])
  <;>
  (try
    {
      aesop
    })

obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := he
  refine' ⟨g, _⟩
  constructor
  · -- Show f ≫ g = id X
    have h₁ := hg
    exact h₁
  · -- Show g ≫ f = id Y
    have h₂ : Mono f := hm
    have h₃ : g ≫ f = id Y := by
      have h₄ : f ≫ g = id X := hg
      have h₅ : ∀ {W} (h k : Hom W X), h ≫ f = k ≫ f → h = k := h₂
      have h₆ : (g ≫ f) ≫ f = (id Y) ≫ f := by
        have h₇ : (g ≫ f) ≫ f = g ≫ (f ≫ f) := by
          simp [comp_assoc]
        have h₈ : (id Y) ≫ f = f := by simp [id_comp]
        have h₉ : g ≫ (f ≫ f) = f := by
          calc
            g ≫ (f ≫ f) = (g ≫ f) ≫ f := by simp [comp_assoc]
            _ = (id Y) ≫ f := by simp [h₄]
            _ = f := by simp [id_comp]
        calc
          (g ≫ f) ≫ f = g ≫ (f ≫ f) := by simp [comp_assoc]
          _ = f := by rw [h₉]
          _ = f := by rfl
          _ = (id Y) ≫ f := by simp [id_comp]
      have h₁₀ : (g ≫ f) ≫ f = (id Y) ≫ f := h₆
      have h₁₁ : g ≫ f = id Y := by
        apply h₂
        simpa [comp_assoc] using h₁₀
      exact h₁₁
    exact h₃

have h : IsIso f := by
    -- Use the fact that f is a split epi to get a left inverse
    obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := he
    -- Show that f is injective (using the mono property)
    refine' ⟨g, _⟩
    -- Verify that f is a right inverse of g
    have h₁ : g ≫ f = id X := by
      calc
        g ≫ f = g ≫ f := rfl
        _ = id X := by
          -- Use the given condition f ≫ g = id X to derive g ≫ f = id X
          have h₂ : f ≫ g = id X := hg
          -- Use the fact that f is mono to cancel f in the composition
          -- f ≫ g = id X implies g ≫ f = id X by canceling f (using mono property)
          have h₃ : g ≫ f = id X := by
            -- Use the cancellative property of mono to prove g ≫ f = id X
            apply hm
            -- Show that (g ≫ f) ≫ f = (id X) ≫ f
            calc
              (g ≫ f) ≫ f = g ≫ (f ≫ f) := by simp [assoc]
              _ = g ≫ (f ≫ f) := rfl
              _ = (id X) ≫ f := by
                -- Use the fact that f ≫ g = id X to get g = g ≫ (f ≫ g)
                have h₄ : f ≫ g = id X := hg
                have h₅ : g = g ≫ (f ≫ g) := by
                  calc
                    g = (id X) ≫ g := by simp [id_comp]
                    _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ g := by rw [h₄]
                    _ = f ≫ (g ≫ g) := by simp [assoc]
                    _ = g ≫ (f ≫ g) := by
                      have h₆ := hm
                      have h₇ := hg
                      simp_all [assoc]
                      <;> simp_all [mono_def]
                      <;>
                      (try aesop)
                      <;>
                      (try simp_all [comp_assoc])
                      <;>
                      (try simp_all [Function.Injective])
                      <;>
                      (try aesop)
                calc
                  g ≫ (f ≫ f) = g ≫ (f ≫ f) := rfl
                  _ = (g ≫ f) ≫ f := by simp [assoc]
                  _ = (g ≫ (f ≫ g)) ≫ f := by rw [h₅]
                  _ = (g ≫ (f ≫ g)) ≫ f := rfl
                  _ = g ≫ ((f ≫ g) ≫ f) := by simp [assoc]
                  _ = g ≫ (id X ≫ f) := by
                    simp_all [h₄]
                  _ = (g ≫ id X) ≫ f := by simp [assoc]
                  _ = g ≫ f := by simp [id_comp]
                  _ = (id X) ≫ f := by
                    have h₈ : g ≫ f = id X := by
                      -- Use the cancellative property of mono to prove g ≫ f = id X
                      apply hm
                      calc
                        (g ≫ f) ≫ f = g ≫ (f ≫ f) := by simp [assoc]
                        _ = (id X) ≫ f := by
                          simp_all [h₄]
                          <;> simp_all [comp_assoc]
                          <;>
                          (try aesop)
                          <;>
                          (try simp_all [Function.Injective])
                          <;>
                          (try aesop)
                        _ = (id X) ≫ f := rfl
                    simp_all [h₈]
                  _ = (id X) ≫ f := rfl
              _ = (id X) ≫ f := rfl
          simp_all
      <;> simp_all [hg]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try simp_all [comp_assoc])
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Function.Injective])
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    -- Conclude that g ≫ f = id X and f ≫ g = id X, hence f is an isomorphism
    exact ⟨by simp_all, by simp_all⟩
  -- The result follows from the above construction
  exact h
